# Close the Loop: Quote → Send → Accept → Invoice (Part 1)

*Generated by /gstack-office-hours on 2026-04-27. Status: APPROVED*

→ 이어지는 내용: [`close-the-loop-2.md`](./close-the-loop-2.md)

---

## Problem Statement

Solo Australian residential painters (sole traders, no staff) spend hours per quote
using spreadsheets. The specific pain: no GST auto-calc, no professional PDF, no way
to send the quote to a customer and track whether they saw it. They quote 5-15 jobs
per month. The lost time is real (15 jobs × ~2 hrs = ~30 hrs/month).

The app (Coatly) already exists: quote builder, PDF generation, invoicing, customer
CRM, schedule, AI drafting, Stripe billing. But the loop is not closed. Email
integration is still being tested. Customers can't accept quotes online. The painter
has no signal on whether their quote was even opened.

---

## Demand Evidence

- Founder domain expertise: personal experience in the painting industry
- Specific pain: spreadsheets taking ~2 hours per quote (confirmed from lived experience)
- Status quo: manual spreadsheet → PDF saved locally → emailed manually → no tracking
- The segment pays: Tradify (~$48/mo) and Ascora (~$29/mo) have thousands of paying
  Australian sole-trader subscribers, proving willingness-to-pay in this market

---

## Status Quo (What Painters Do Today)

1. Site visit → rough notes on phone
2. Home → open spreadsheet → manually calculate room areas × paint coverage × labour
3. Add GST manually (often wrong)
4. Export to PDF or screenshot
5. Email from personal Gmail
6. Wait. No idea if the customer opened it.
7. Call to follow up. Awkward. Often too late.

---

## Target User & Narrowest Wedge

**User:** Jim, 35, sole trader residential painter in Western Sydney. ABN registered.
No staff. Quotes 10-15 jobs per month. Uses an iPhone on job sites. Not technical —
Google Maps and texting is roughly the ceiling.

**Wedge:** "Create a professional quote in 10 minutes instead of 2 hours."

---

## Constraints

- Mobile-first PWA (Next.js + Tailwind). Touch targets 44px+, key actions at screen bottom.
- Serverless on Vercel. React-PDF only (no Puppeteer).
- RLS required on all Supabase queries.
- Monetary values in cents integers, no `any` types.
- Credit-card-first model — deliberate choice to filter for serious users.

---

## Premises

1. Solo residential painters waste ~30 hrs/month on spreadsheet quoting and would
   pay to fix it. **[AGREED]** — the segment pays for trade software (Tradify, Ascora).

2. The wedge is speed-to-quote, not job management or scheduling. **[AGREED]** — the
   schedule feature (764-line component) was already removed. Core loop is right.

3. Getting a painter to their first *accepted* quote in one session is the activation
   metric. **[AGREED]** — with CC-first model, session 1 must deliver this.

4. Good/better/best tier pricing as a differentiator. **[NOT ENDORSED — DEFERRED]** —
   Not a build priority.

---

## Approaches Considered

### Approach A: Close the Loop (CHOSEN)
Complete the quote → send → customer-accepts → invoice chain end-to-end.
Effort: S (~1-2 weeks). Risk: Low.
Pros: proves core value prop in production; read receipt is the "aha moment"; all
infrastructure already exists (Resend, /q/, PDF).

### Approach B: Activation-First Redesign
Redesign onboarding + first-use so painter creates and sends first quote in < 10 min.
Effort: M (~3-4 weeks). Risk: Medium.

### Approach C: AI as the Hook
Move AI quote drafting from Pro-only to "3 free AI quotes/month" on Starter.
Effort: M (~3-4 weeks). Risk: Medium-High.

---

## Recommended Approach: A — Close the Loop

### Priority 1: Complete Email Send Flow (Resend)

- From quote detail page: one-tap "Send Quote to Customer"
- Generates PDF → attaches to Resend email → delivers to customer email
- Email contains the /q/ public link for online viewing
- Status updates: quote status → "sent" immediately on successful Resend API response

**Debugging checklist for current instability:**
  - Verify Resend API key is set in Vercel env vars (not just local .env)
  - Confirm sending domain is verified in Resend dashboard (SPF + DKIM records)
  - Check Resend logs for bounce/rejection reason on test sends
  - Confirm `from` address matches the verified domain (not a personal Gmail)
  - Test with a non-Gmail recipient first (Gmail has aggressive spam filtering)

**PDF delivery:** Generate PDF → upload to Supabase Storage (public bucket, permanent URL) →
send Storage URL in email body (not as an attachment).
  - Use a public bucket (not private + signed URL) so the link never expires.
  - Vercel function timeout: React-PDF generation + Storage upload must complete within 10s.
  - Error handling: if Storage upload fails, do NOT update quote status.

### Priority 2: Customer-Facing Quote Accept

- /q/[id] public page: professional quote display, no login required
- "Accept This Quote" button → triggers status update to "approved"

**Security:** Accept action uses a signed token embedded in the /q/ URL.
  - Pattern: `/q/[id]?token=[hmac]`
  - Algorithm: HMAC-SHA256(quote_id, `QUOTE_ACCEPT_SECRET` env var)
  - Token generated at email send time and included in the URL sent to customer
  - Server validates: `expectedToken === crypto.createHmac('sha256', secret).update(quoteId).digest('hex')`
  - Known limitation: token appears in server logs and Resend click-tracking. Acceptable
    for v1 (this is a quote accept, not a payment). Mitigate: avoid logging /q/ query params.
  - Token does NOT expire for v1 (quote expiry is handled via `valid_until` field check).

**Idempotency:** Accept button disabled + shows "Quote Accepted" after first click.
Server-side: only update status if current status is "sent".

**Expired quote handling:** Before accepting, check `valid_until < now()`. If expired:
return 422, show customer: "This quote has expired. Please contact the painter."

**Painter notification on accept:** Resend email to painter's registered email with
subject "Quote #[number] accepted by [customer name]". Fire-and-forget.

**What status transitions are valid:** draft → sent (on email dispatch), sent → approved
(on customer accept), sent → rejected (future), sent → expired (cron job, future).
